rankings.title: Do Low-Emission Zones Gentrify? Vehicle Bans, Air Quality, and Housing Price Capitalization in French Cities
conditions: reframing the core claim as capitalization/incidence unless composition data are added
using repeat-sales or parcel-level specifications plus strong boundary/event-study placebo tests
documenting a credible air-quality first stage with official monitor or satellite data

rankings.title: Do Fiber Broadband Networks Fuel Populism? France's Plan France Tres Haut Debit
conditions: turning the rollout rules into a true threshold/RD or other quasi-experimental design
measuring take-up
not just availability
showing strong pre-trend/placebo evidence around political outcomes

rankings.title: Russia's Gas Cutoff and European Industrial Restructuring
Idea 1: Do Low-Emission Zones Gentrify? Vehicle Bans, Air Quality, and Housing Price Capitalization in French Cities
conditions: demonstrating that ZFE boundaries do not perfectly overlap with pre-existing demographic fault lines
including a robust test for traffic/pollution spillovers just outside the zone

rankings.title: Idea 3: Do Fiber Broadband Networks Fuel Populism? France's Plan France Tres Haut Debit
Idea 2: Russia's Gas Cutoff and European Industrial Restructuring
Do Low-Emission Zones Gentrify? Vehicle Bans, Air Quality, and Housing Price Capitalization in French Cities
conditions: using a tighter design than a simple inside/outside DiD
adding repeat-sales/parcel FE
strong placebo tests
either measuring composition/displacement directly with auxiliary census data or reframing from “gentrification” to “capitalization/incidence”

rankings.title: Russia's Gas Cutoff and European Industrial Restructuring
conditions: upgrading to a country-sector triple-diff
carefully addressing endogenous exposure
differential national policy responses
showing that results are not driven by a few high-exposure countries
